title: Aerangis laurentii
taxonomy: Aerangis laurentii is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which comprises the orchid plant family. This family is further divided into subfamilies, with Aerangis laurentii being part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. Aerangis laurentii is also classified within the subtribe Vandeae. The genus Aerangis is a monophyletic group, indicating a common evolutionary origin for its species. Aerangis laurentii shares characteristics with other Aerangis species, which are primarily epiphytic orchids native to Africa.

intro: Aerangis laurentii is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical forests of West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Aerangis laurentii contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aerangis laurentii include infusions and tinctures, often used in traditional remedies to soothe respiratory tract infections and boost the immune system.
